body.title: Granny dispatch gate: add 7d utilization check alongside 5h
body.result: Implemented by linter during daemon.py edits: _check_rate_limit returns 3-tuple, checks both 5h and 7d thresholds, run_once updated.
body.description:
Granny's dispatch gate currently pauses on 5h utilization ≥ 90% (GRANNY_RATE_LIMIT_PAUSE). The 7d-all-models utilization is equally important for deciding whether to dispatch overnight — a 5h window at 2% can still be a bad time to dispatch if the weekly budget is nearly exhausted. Add a second configurable threshold (GRANNY_RATE_LIMIT_7D_PAUSE, default 90%) that reads `usage.seven_day.utilization` from ~/.claude/usage-cache.json, and log which signal (5h or 7d) triggered the pause.

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** In scope: read 7d utilization from usage cache, add env-var threshold, log which signal fired. Out of scope: extra_usage credits tracking, any new cache-writing logic, UI.
**Completion criteria:** (1) With GRANNY_RATE_LIMIT_7D_PAUSE=0, Granny logs '7d rate limit at X%' and returns 0 from run_once(). (2) With both thresholds untripped, dispatch proceeds normally. (3) Log line names which signal (5h or 7d) triggered the pause.
